wrongWhy: This is inspection costs, warranty repair costs, and spoilage costs. Inspection costs are appraisal costs, not internal failure costs; and warranty repair costs are external failure costs, not internal failure costs.
This is warranty repair costs, liability claims costs, and rework costs. Warranty repair costs and liability claims costs are external failure costs, not internal failure costs.
This is inspection costs and rework costs. inspection is an appraisal cost, not an internal failure cost. 18.

4-21.q: When evaluating the cost of quality in an organization, which one of the following would be considered an internal failure cost?
o: The cost to inspect units produced
The cost to rework defective units
4-21.e: Internal failure occurs when a problem is detected before a defective product is shipped to a customer. Reworking defective units is done when they are found to be defective before they are sold or shipped, so the cost of rework is an internal failure cost.
wrongWhy: Internal failure occurs when a problem is detected before a defective product is shipped to a customer. Warranty repair costs are costs to correct a defect after the customer has received the product, so they are external failure costs.

Internal failure occurs when a problem is detected before a defective product is shipped to a customer. Inspection of units must occur before a problem can be detected. The cost to inspect units produced is an appraisal cost, which is a cost incurred in order to determine if an individual unit is defective.

Internal failure occurs when a problem is detected before a defective product is shipped to a customer. Product testing may be done before a new product enters into production, so that would be part of the product design cost. Product design is a prevention cost, because flaws in design become defective products. Quality design and testing prevent defective products from ever being produced. Product testing might also be considered an appraisal cost, if it refers to testing or inspecting the finished product at the completion of production. Both prevention costs and appraisal costs are conformance costs, whereas internal failure costs are nonconformance costs. 19.

4-21.q: All of the following are examples of prevention costs except:
o: Supervision of the testing and inspection activities
Quality data gathering, analysis and reporting
4-21.e: Supervision of testing and inspection activities is an example of appraisal costs. Appraisal costs include the cost of any activity designed to test, appraise, or check for defective products.
wrongWhy: This is an example of a prevention cost. Prevention costs are the cost of any quality activity designed to do the job right the first time and to minimize the cost of failures (both internal and external). Quality training contributes to those goals.

This is an example of a prevention cost. Prevention costs are the cost of any quality activity designed to do the job right the first time and to minimize the cost of failures (both internal and external). Quality data gathering, analysis, and reporting ensures that quality is within the tolerance limits.

This is an example of a prevention cost. Prevention costs are the costs of any quality activity designed to do the job right the first time and to minimize the cost of failures (both internal and external). Technical support provided to suppliers would help them to manufacture and supply products of the right quality the very first time, thereby reducing the incidence of rework or customer returns. 20.

4-21.q: The use of quality circles working as teams in total quality management is important because:
o: The use of quality circles working as teams eliminates the need for supervision, thereby allowing a company to become leaner and more profitable
Employee motivation is higher for team members in a quality circle than for individual contributors
Well-managed quality circles working as teams can be highly creative and are able to address complex problems better than individuals can
Quality circles are quicker to make decisions, thereby helping to reduce cycle time
4-21.e: Quality circles working as teams can use the diverse knowledge and skills of all team members.
wrongWhy: Although need for supervision may be reduced, it is not eliminated.
Although employee motivation may be high in quality circles working as teams, the high motivation does not always translate directly to quality improvement.
Quality circles working as teams are often inefficient and costly. 1.
